scapegoat.name: capitalist production
attributes: periodically reduces wages during crisis and stagnation
exploits intensified labour without proportional reduction in working hours
treats labour as a commodity subject to market fluctuations
maintains systemic inequality through cyclical economic control
scapegoat.justification: The text identifies capitalism as the driving force behind wage suppression during downturns and profit maximization during booms, framing it as inherently exploitative and structurally rigged against workers.

moral_justification: Workers are systematically degraded by capitalist structures that treat labour as a tradable commodity, forcing them into cycles of dependency and degradation without agency or fair compensation, especially when wage gains are negated by increased intensity or economic downturns.
summary: This passage deconstructs the capitalist mode of production by exposing how wage struggles are reactive responses to structural exploitation, not independent choices. It condemns capitalism for commodifying labour, manipulating working hours and wages through industrial cycles, and denying workers stability, while offering no constructive alternative — only moral indictment.
